subject: Mathematics
title: What Is a Prime Number?
description: A fast breakdown of What Is a Prime Number? for builders.

hook.text: Some numbers refuse to be divided. They only split by 1 and themselves.

buildup.text: 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13. These are primes. They thin out but never stop appearing.

discovery.text: Euclid proved around 300 BC that primes continue forever. His proof still holds.

twist.text: 2 is the only even prime. Every other even number can be split in half.

climax.text: Primes are the atoms of arithmetic. Every whole number is a unique product of primes.

punchline.text: All of math is built on numbers that refuse to break.

quiz.question: What makes a number prime?
options: It can only be divided by 1 and itself
